subject: Ecology
title: Hydrothermal Vents
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Hydrothermal Vents.

hook.text: In 1977, scientists found thriving ecosystems around volcanic vents on the ocean floor.

buildup.text: Superheated water laden with minerals shoots from cracks in the Earth's crust.

discovery.text: Bacteria here use chemosynthesis—harvesting chemical energy instead of sunlight.

twist.text: Giant tube worms, blind shrimp, and ghostly crabs thrive in total darkness and toxic water.

climax.text: Vents may resemble conditions where life on Earth first began billions of years ago.

punchline.text: Life started in darkness and poison.

quiz.question: How do bacteria at hydrothermal vents get energy?
